rationale: A0 and EV-003 show Apache ActiveMQ (java.exe) spawning cmd.exe which uses certutil to download a randomly-named executable (qSwUwejx.exe) from an internal attacker-controlled host (10.0.2.13:8080), consistent with exploitation of CVE-2023-46604 or similar ActiveMQ RCE. EV-006 shows the dropped payload executing at High integrity from the Temp directory, and EV-007 confirms it immediately established a TCP connection to 10.0.2.13:4444 — a classic Metasploit/reverse-shell port — indicating successful C2 establishment. EV-008/EV-009 show the payload subsequently spawning an interactive cmd.exe shell (with a null parent GUID, indicating process injection or hollowing), and EV-011/EV-012 reveal the attacker then used that shell to download AnyDesk via certutil, a well-known persistence and lateral-movement technique for establishing a legitimate-looking remote access channel.
